note_evidence: Mr. Isreal Howell is an 85-year-old man presenting for his annual comprehensive examination. He reports feeling well.
rationale: Age, annual exam, and feeling well all stated.

note_evidence: He denies chest pain or pressure at rest or with usual exertion, noting only an occasional brief twinge with heavy yard work that resolves promptly with rest
rationale: No anginal pain with exertional twinge relieved by rest documented.

note_evidence: he has not needed his nitroglycerin spray in many months
rationale: Directly stated.

note_evidence: He denies exertional dyspnea, orthopnea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, palpitations, lower-extremity edema
rationale: Dyspnea, edema, and PND all denied.

note_evidence: denies exertional dyspnea, orthopnea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, palpitations, lower-extremity edema, lightheadedness, syncope, and falls over the past year
rationale: Lightheadedness and falls denied over past year.

note_evidence: Appetite is preserved and weight is stable to slightly down.
rationale: Appetite good and weight steady documented.

note_evidence: He retired from a sixty-year bookkeeping career this June ... Not in labor force newly documented following retirement this year.
rationale: Retirement in June after 60-year career and out of labor force documented.

note_evidence: he lives with his daughter and grandson (three in the household)
rationale: Household composition and size stated.

note_evidence: denies difficulty affording food, medications, or transportation
rationale: No unmet financial needs documented.
No PRAPARE, insurance, housing, contact frequency, or stress level details in note.

note_evidence: Medication reconciliation performed with his pill bottles
rationale: Reconciliation with brought-in bottles documented.

note_evidence: duplicate simvastatin 20 mg entry from a prior hospitalization removed
rationale: Simvastatin 20 mg duplicate from prior hospitalization removed.

note_evidence: metoprolol succinate ER 100 mg daily (outdated 50 mg ER entry removed)
rationale: Outdated metoprolol 50 mg entry removed.

note_evidence: Consolidate to atorvastatin 20 mg nightly
rationale: Continuation of atorvastatin 20 mg nightly documented.

note_evidence: Continue losartan 50 mg daily and metoprolol succinate ER 100 mg daily
rationale: Metoprolol ER 100 mg continued (note states daily rather than morning).

note_evidence: Continue aspirin 81 mg daily
rationale: Aspirin continuation documented.

note_evidence: Continue losartan 50 mg daily
rationale: Losartan continuation documented.

note_evidence: nitroglycerin 0.4 mg/actuation mucosal spray PRN chest pain
rationale: Nitroglycerin spray PRN continued.

note_evidence: Start 24 hr metformin hydrochloride 500 mg extended-release tablet daily with the evening meal
rationale: Metformin ER 500 mg daily with dinner started.

note_evidence: Start hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg each morning
rationale: HCTZ 25 mg each morning started in setting of elevated potassium/variable BP.

note_evidence: Counseled to avoid skipping breakfast before morning walks; report GI intolerance rather than self-discontinuing
rationale: Counsel to report GI intolerance rather than stopping documented.

note_evidence: Start hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg each morning ... Monitor for orthostatic symptoms
rationale: Morning dosing and orthostatic monitoring documented.

rationale: Heart rate stated.

rationale: Low fasting glucose and breakfast counsel documented.

note_evidence: creatinine 0.6 mg/dL; eGFR 148.95 mL/min/1.73m2
rationale: Creatinine and strong renal function documented.

note_evidence: Potassium 5.16 mmol/L at the upper margin on losartan
rationale: Mildly elevated potassium documented.

note_evidence: total cholesterol 226.05 mg/dL; direct LDL 148.61 mg/dL ... above goal for secondary prevention
rationale: Cholesterol and LDL values above goal given cardiac history.

rationale: Recheck lipids before statin change documented.
